layout.pageDescription: Thirdspace at the University of Toronto. A research group studying human-computer interaction, AI ethics, and communities at the margins.

hero.subParagraph: Building socio-technical systems for responsible, participatory, and context-aware AI with communities too often left outside the room where technology is designed.
hero.title: Utilizing human values, situated knowledge, and experiences for developing impactful solutions.
hero.description: The Third Space research group, led by Prof. Ishtiaque Ahmed at the University of Toronto, studies the social, ethical, and political dimensions of computing and artificial intelligence.
hero.researchPostureBody: Technology is never neutral; we study who it serves, who it misses, and how it can be remade.

groupOverview.headline: Computing research from the social edge inward.
groupOverview.body: The Third Space research group, led by Prof. Ishtiaque Ahmed at the University of Toronto, studies the social, ethical, and political dimensions of computing and artificial intelligence. The group works at the intersection of human-computer interaction (HCI), AI, and critical computing to understand how technologies shape—and are shaped by—everyday life practices, particularly in contexts of marginalization and global development, centering communities that are often overlooked in mainstream computing research.

about.title: About the Group
about.body: Through empirical and design-oriented research, the group develops and studies socio-technical systems that support more responsible, participatory, and context-aware forms of AI and digital technologies. Their work is grounded in collaboration with communities and draws on interdisciplinary perspectives to reimagine how computing systems can better serve diverse human values and needs.

pillars.title: Empirical Research
pillars.body: Through empirical and design-oriented research, we develop and study socio-technical systems that support more responsible, participatory, and context-aware forms of AI and digital technologies.

pillars.title: Community Grounding
pillars.body: Our work is grounded in collaboration with communities and draws on interdisciplinary perspectives to reimagine how computing systems can better serve diverse human values.

pillars.title: Responsible Tech
pillars.body: Developing impactful systems that prioritize marginalization contexts and centering communities often overlooked in mainstream computing.
